User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the CampusConnect platform, including students, faculty, and administrators.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for login and notifications.

role.description: The role of the user within the platform (e.g., 'student', 'faculty', 'admin').

departmentId.description: Reference to the Department this user belongs to. (Relationship: Department 1:N User)

Department.title: Department
Department.description: Represents an academic or administrative department within the college.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Department entity.

name.description: The full name of the department (e.g., 'Computer Science Engineering').

code.description: A short code or abbreviation for the department (e.g., 'CSE').

description.description: A brief description of the department.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents an academic course offered by a department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

name.description: The title or name of the course (e.g., 'Data Structures and Algorithms').

departmentId.description: Reference to the Department offering this course. (Relationship: Department 1:N Course)

credits.description: The number of academic credits awarded for completing the course.

Enrollment.title: Enrollment
Enrollment.description: Represents a student's enrollment in a specific course for a given semester and year.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student user enrolled in the course. (Relationship: User 1:N Enrollment)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course in which the student is enrolled. (Relationship: Course 1:N Enrollment)

semester.description: The academic semester of the enrollment (e.g., 'Fall', 'Spring', 'Summer').

year.description: The academic year of the enrollment.

Attendance.title: Attendance
Attendance.description: Records the attendance status of a student for a specific course session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attendance entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student user whose attendance is being recorded. (Relationship: User 1:N Attendance)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course for which attendance is recorded. (Relationship: Course 1:N Attendance)

date.description: The date and time when the attendance was marked.

markedByFacultyId.description: Reference to the Faculty user who marked the attendance. (Relationship: User 1:N Attendance)

TimetableEntry.title: TimetableEntry
TimetableEntry.description: Represents a single entry in the academic timetable for classes, exams, or events.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TimetableEntry entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course associated with this timetable entry. (Relationship: Course 1:N TimetableEntry)

facultyId.description: Reference to the Faculty user assigned to this timetable entry. (Relationship: User 1:N TimetableEntry)

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week for the timetable entry (e.g., 'Monday', 'Tuesday').

startTime.description: The start time of the session (e.g., '09:00 AM').

endTime.description: The end time of the session (e.g., '10:00 AM').

room.description: The physical room or location where the session takes place.

semester.description: The academic semester for this timetable entry.

year.description: The academic year for this timetable entry.

Result.title: Result
Result.description: Stores the academic results for a student in a specific course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Result entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student user associated with this result. (Relationship: User 1:N Result)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course for which the result is recorded. (Relationship: Course 1:N Result)

semester.description: The academic semester of the result.

year.description: The academic year of the result.

marks.description: The numerical marks obtained by the student in the course.

grade.description: The letter grade awarded to the student (e.g., 'A', 'B+', 'C').

published.description: Indicates whether the result has been officially published and is visible to students.

Assignment.title: Assignment
Assignment.description: Defines an academic assignment for a particular course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assignment entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this assignment belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Assignment)

description.description: A detailed description of the assignment requirements.

deadline.description: The submission deadline for the assignment.

fileUrl.description: URL to any supplementary assignment files or instructions.

Submission.title: Submission
Submission.description: Records a student's submission for a given assignment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Submission entity.

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment this submission is for. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N Submission)

studentId.description: Reference to the Student user who made the submission. (Relationship: User 1:N Submission)

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the assignment was submitted.

fileUrl.description: URL to the submitted assignment file.

marksAwarded.description: The marks awarded for the submission, if graded.

StudyMaterial.title: StudyMaterial
StudyMaterial.description: Provides access to study resources and materials for courses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudyMaterial entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this material is relevant to. (Relationship: Course 1:N StudyMaterial)

title.description: The title of the study material.

description.description: A brief description of the study material.

fileUrl.description: URL to the study material file (e.g., PDF, presentation).

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (faculty/admin) who uploaded the material. (Relationship: User 1:N StudyMaterial)

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the material was uploaded.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Communicates important messages to specific target audiences within the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

title.description: The title or subject of the announcement.

description.description: The full content of the announcement.

targetAudience.description: The intended audience for the announcement (e.g., 'all', 'students', 'faculty').

date.description: The date and time when the announcement was posted.

postedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (admin/faculty) who posted the announcement. (Relationship: User 1:N Announcement)

Forum.title: Forum
Forum.description: Represents an online discussion forum, potentially linked to a specific course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Forum entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this forum is associated with. (Relationship: Course 1:N Forum)

description.description: A description of the forum's purpose or topic.

createdByUser.description: Reference to the User who created the forum. (Relationship: User 1:N Forum)

createdAt.description: The date and time when the forum was created.

ForumPost.title: ForumPost
ForumPost.description: Represents an individual post within a discussion forum.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ForumPost entity.

forumId.description: Reference to the Forum this post belongs to. (Relationship: Forum 1:N ForumPost)

userId.description: Reference to the User who authored this post. (Relationship: User 1:N ForumPost)

content.description: The textual content of the forum post.

postedAt.description: The date and time when the post was created.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Describes an event happening at the college, such as workshops, cultural fests, or seminars.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the event, including agenda and purpose.

time.description: The time of the event (e.g., '10:00 AM - 12:00 PM').

location.description: The physical or virtual location where the event will take place.

organizerName.description: The name of the organizer (e.g., 'CSE Department', 'Photography Club').

Club.title: Club
Club.description: Represents a student club or organization at the college.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Club entity.

name.description: The full name of the club.

description.description: A description of the club's activities and mission.

facultyInchargeId.description: Reference to the Faculty member who is in charge of the club. (Relationship: User 1:N Club)

ClubMembership.title: ClubMembership
ClubMembership.description: Links a student user to a club and defines their role within it.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ClubMembership entity.

clubId.description: Reference to the Club the student is a member of. (Relationship: Club 1:N ClubMembership)

studentId.description: Reference to the Student user who is a member. (Relationship: User 1:N ClubMembership)

role.description: The role of the student within the club (e.g., 'Member', 'Secretary', 'President').

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Manages notifications sent to individual users about various activities or updates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who is the recipient of this notification. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

message.description: The content of the notification message.

read.description: Indicates whether the user has read the notification.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the notification was created.

definition.description: Stores user profiles including roles and departmental affiliations. The `id` matches `request.auth.uid`. The `role` field is used for DBAC.
Contains information about academic departments. Accessible broadly, editable by admin.
Courses offered by a specific department. `departmentId` is in the path for clear hierarchy.
Student-specific enrollments in courses. `studentId` is in the path. `courseId` is denormalized in the document for faculty collection group queries.
Records student attendance. `studentId` in path for student access. `courseId` and `markedByFacultyId` are denormalized for faculty collection group queries.

Timetable entries for specific courses. `courseId` is in the path. `facultyId` is denormalized in the document, crucial for faculty to query their assigned timetable entries via a collection group query without using `get()`.
Academic results for students. `studentId` in path. `courseId` and `published` status are denormalized for faculty collection group queries and explicit state modeling.
Assignments posted for a specific course. `courseId` is in the path.
Student submissions for assignments. `studentId` is in the path. `assignmentId` and `courseId` are denormalized in the document for faculty collection group queries for grading.
Study materials for a specific course. `courseId` in path. `uploadedByUserId` denormalized for authorization independence.
Global or targeted announcements. `postedByUserId` and `targetAudience` are denormalized to allow secure list operations based on user role and audience without `get()`.
Discussion forums associated with a course. `courseId` in path. `createdByUser` denormalized.
Individual posts within a course forum. `courseId` and `forumId` in path. `userId` denormalized for clear ownership.
Information about college events. Publicly readable.
Student clubs and organizations. `facultyInchargeId` denormalized for authorization independence (e.g., faculty can manage their club).
Links students to clubs. `clubId` in path. `studentId` and `role` are denormalized for student collection group queries (viewing their own memberships) and club management.
Individual notifications for users. `userId` is in the path, enabling secure and efficient retrieval of a user's notifications (QAP).

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure for CampusConnect is designed adhering to the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling, ensuring a secure, scalable, and debuggable database.The primary strategy for Authorization Independence is **denormalization of authorization context**. Key IDs and role-related fields are duplicated into child documents where access control is required. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules, which also enables atomic operations and simplifies rule debugging.For example, in collections like `users/{studentId}/attendance` or `users/{studentId}/submissions`, the `studentId` is part of the path, providing immediate ownership context. Additionally, fields like `courseId` and `facultyId` (e.g., in `TimetableEntry`) or `published` (in `Result`) are denormalized into the document itself. This allows for secure collection group queries without `get()` operations, directly addressing the console error related to `TimetableEntry` and `facultyId`.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives)** are enabled through several structural choices:
1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For user-specific data (e.g., attendance, results, notifications, personal enrollments, submissions), data is nested directly under `/users/{userId}/...`. This allows students to query `their` data directly and efficiently, with security rules simply checking `request.auth.uid == userId` in the path. This supports secure `list` operations for personal data.
2.  **Collection Group Queries with Denormalized Fields:** For faculty needing to view all `TimetableEntry`, `Attendance`, `Enrollment`, `Result`, or `Submission` documents across various courses they teach, these entities are structured as subcollections. Critical fields like `facultyId`, `courseId`, and `studentId` are denormalized within each document. This allows faculty to perform collection group queries filtered by `facultyId` or a list of `courseId`s (`where('facultyId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `where('courseId', 'in', facultyCourseIds)`). Similarly, students can query `timetableEntries` or `clubMemberships` that are relevant to them using collection group queries filtered by their `studentId` or `courseId`s.
3.  **Structural Segregation:** Collections like `/announcements` use an explicit `targetAudience` field, allowing rules to filter visibility based on user roles without needing separate physical collections for each audience, while still maintaining homogeneous security within the collection (all documents are announcements, but their `read` permission is role-dependent).

**DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control)** is implemented by storing user roles directly in the `/users/{userId}` documents. Security rules will read the `role` field from the authenticated user's document to determine permissions. For example, an `admin` user would have broad permissions, while `faculty` would manage specific courses, and `students` would mostly view their own or publicly available data.

In summary, the design prioritizes atomic rule evaluation by minimizing cross-document lookups, facilitates efficient and secure list queries through strategic path design and denormalization, and simplifies rule logic by embedding authorization context directly within the data structure.
